content_of_post_to_comment: This sounds a little patronizing, and as you get older, you realize that you have to let people live their live their way without judgement. I basically grew on these types of quotes - be your own boss, travel the world; and to be fair that is all solid advice, as is this one. I started my business, travelled everywhere before I turned 35, smelled the roses - the whole song-and-dance thing. But along the way I also realized that those doing 9 to 5 are not to be looked down upon. If someone is not travelling, going to office and back home, raising a family, and retiring at 65, that is a solid life too and we are nobody to judge whether that is a wasted life. Maybe that person is absolutely happy with their lives. My idea of a good life is trying new cuisines, meeting new people, taking risks; but maybe there is someone else who is just as satisfied staying home and knitting.
